title: Build book details screen and complete end-to-end QA
goal: Display scanned book information cleanly and verify the full Laravel-to-React-Native flow.
implementation_steps: Display author, category, library reference number, ISBN, status, available copies, total copies, and cover image.
If cover image is missing, show a clean placeholder.
If ISBN is missing, show '-' or 'Not provided'.
Show availability clearly using available_quantity and quantity.
If status is unavailable, visually indicate that no copies are available.
If recent_transactions exists, show a staff-only recent borrowing section.
Do not show recent_transactions for User/borrower.
Add a button to scan another book.
Add README setup steps for MobileApp.
Run Expo app in Expo Go on a phone.
Verify backend connection using LAN IP or ngrok.
Document any required local network setup.
readme_required_sections: How to run with Expo Go
How to point to local Laravel backend
final_qa_checklist: Backend runs with php artisan serve --host=0.0.0.0 --port=8000 or equivalent LAN-accessible setup.
MobileApp .env points to http://YOUR_LOCAL_IP:8000/api or ngrok URL.
Register creates a new User/borrower account.
Valid QR scan displays book details.
Admin/librarian can see recent borrowing metadata when backend returns it.
acceptance_criteria: Book details render correctly after QR scan.
UI handles missing image and missing ISBN gracefully.
Staff-only transaction metadata appears only for admin/librarian.
User/borrower sees basic book details only.
MobileApp README is complete enough for another developer to run the app.
Expo Go manual test passes on a physical phone.
